title: The Night You Left
content: The apartment felt unusually quiet after you closed the door behind you. Nothing dramatic happened. No shouting. No broken promises. Just the sound of your footsteps fading down the hallway until they disappeared completely. For several days, I expected to hear your keys turning in the lock again. Your favorite mug remained untouched on the shelf, and your books stayed exactly where you had left them. It took time to understand that people sometimes leave long before they physically walk away. Their absence begins in conversations that grow shorter, in smiles that become tired, and in dreams that slowly stop including both names. Looking back now, I no longer remember that night as the end of our story. I remember it as the evening when I slowly began learning how to rebuild my life without expecting someone else to complete it.
